rationale: The reviewed art/general grouping intentionally uses the familiar headword as a category label before a basic contrastive explanation.
Cambridge B1 evidence is for the noun, while this card identity is a verb.
Adjective and adverb share one compact B1 learning unit and one sense.
The four senses are close applications of ending a connection.
The senses remain organized around restricted access or inclusion.
Professional status and skill level remain distinct learner-facing senses.
The physical headpiece and institutional royal power remain distinct.
Granting a right and assigning a title are independent constructions.
Public honour and magnificent beauty remain distinct senses.
The shop and the goods sold there are distinct uses.
False belief and deceptive appearance are distinct mental and visual senses.
Non-alignment and absence of a strong quality remain distinct.
A question and an editorial question-mark annotation remain distinct.
Forceful capture, official confiscation, and opportunity-taking remain distinct.
The grouped physical sense and the stricter-policy sense remain independent despite sharing the first gloss token.
Instruction and the fees charged for it remain distinct.
Mortal is the clearest basic adjective for the reviewed human-finiteness sense; rewriting would reduce precision.
